raw_response: {"response":"Reject the submitted workflow. It treats the fulfillment metrics dashboard as chart review and refresh work instead of helping the operations lead decide which exception needs follow-up today. It omits the exception meaning, owner, freshness, and next action that would make the fulfillment metrics actionable.","next_action":"Repair and resubmit by reframing the dashboard around exception follow-up: for each fulfillment exception, show what it means, who owns it, how fresh the data is, what status or dependency could change the decision, and the next action. Add a validation or follow-up path so the operations lead can check current status before deciding which exception needs follow-up today.","questions":[],"handoff":"Decision: Reject; Owner: operations lead; Action: repair the fulfillment metrics dashboard into an exception follow-up monitor; Reason: charts and refresh controls do not identify which exception needs follow-up today; Required fix: show exception meaning, owner, freshness, decision-changing status, next action, and a validation path before handoff.","rationale":"The workflow does not match the source request because it stops at viewing fulfillment metrics rather than supporting exception triage. The operations lead could make a follow-up decision without seeing freshness or ownership, and cannot evaluate current exception status before committing to the next action."}

parsed.response: Reject the submitted workflow. It treats the fulfillment metrics dashboard as chart review and refresh work instead of helping the operations lead decide which exception needs follow-up today. It omits the exception meaning, owner, freshness, and next action that would make the fulfillment metrics actionable.
parsed.next_action: Repair and resubmit by reframing the dashboard around exception follow-up: for each fulfillment exception, show what it means, who owns it, how fresh the data is, what status or dependency could change the decision, and the next action. Add a validation or follow-up path so the operations lead can check current status before deciding which exception needs follow-up today.
parsed.handoff: Decision: Reject; Owner: operations lead; Action: repair the fulfillment metrics dashboard into an exception follow-up monitor; Reason: charts and refresh controls do not identify which exception needs follow-up today; Required fix: show exception meaning, owner, freshness, decision-changing status, next action, and a validation path before handoff.
parsed.rationale: The workflow does not match the source request because it stops at viewing fulfillment metrics rather than supporting exception triage. The operations lead could make a follow-up decision without seeing freshness or ownership, and cannot evaluate current exception status before committing to the next action.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
